title: Rachana Lalit Talekar, M.P.H., B.D.S.
text:
I am a digital health and implementation science researcher with a background in community health, program design, and telehealth innovation. As Research Program Manager, I lead funded research projects, manage research and education initiatives and support interdisciplianary grant writing efforts aimed at delivering scalable, sustainable healthcare solutions in underserved communities. My work bridges technology, research, and community need through integration of user-centered technology and behavioral health strategies to enhance access and outcomes. With prior experience developing open-source digital health curricula for global health programs and directing telehealth platform deployments, I bring both global perspective and local impact to my work. I am also currently pursuing my Ph.D. in Health Education at Texas A&M University, with research focusing on systems science modeling of telehealth utilization patterns in rural communities.
  








  2022 – Present
  
    Ph.D. Candidate
    Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.)
    Health Education | Health Behavior
    Texas A&M University School of Public Health, College Station, TX, USA
  



  2020
  
    M.P.H.
    Master of Public Health (M.P.H.)
    Program Designing, Implementation and Evaluation
    Boston University School of Public Health, Boston, MA, USA
  



  2016
  
    B.D.S.
    Bachelor of Dental Surgery (B.D.S.)
    Dental Surgery
    Terna Dental College, Maharashtra University of Health Sciences, Navi Mumbai, India

title: Professional Experience
text:
Program Manager
    Project Coordinator III
    Project Coordinator I
    Editorial Coordinator
  

  
  

    
      
        Program Manager
        August 2024 – Present
      
      Texas A&M Health Telehealth Institute, College Station, TX

      Research Program Manager
      
        Support data collection activities by organizing and managing necessary resources and personnel.
        Manage grant writing processes, including drafting proposals, literature review, and budgetary processes.
        Plan and assist with Institutional Review Board (IRB) submissions, preparing and submitting all necessary documentation.
        Liaise with Texas A&M University research services to coordinate efforts and ensure compliance with university policies.
        Coordinate within project teams and research staff to ensure alignment towards tasks and goals.
        Prepare detailed budgets and supporting documents to ensure transparent and well-documented financial aspects of projects.
        Assist in developing strategic plans and goals to support the Research Core, aligning with its mission and objectives.
      

      Education Program Manager
      
        Lead graduate public health practicum experience, including site coordination, student selection, and competency alignment.
        Serve as preceptor and supervisor for student interns, providing mentorship, feedback, and structured guidance.
        Facilitate the Telehealth Student Fellows program for multi-disciplinary PhD students.
        Maintain detailed program documentation including practicum agreements, student evaluations, and competency tracking tools.
        Foster sustained partnerships across university academic units to develop telehealth curriculum material.
        Contribute to strategic planning for the Education Core by supporting program growth in telehealth and applied public health practice.
      
    

    
      
        Project Coordinator III (Technology Lead)
        Mar 2022 – July 2024
      
      Texas Child Health Access Through Telemedicine (TCHATT), Texas A&M School of Medicine, College Station, TX
      
        Facilitated the development, design, procurement, installation, and implementation of telehealth services including hardware, software, and associated technology systems.
        Led multidisciplinary teams across multiple states in the delivery of child mental health services.
        Assessed program needs related to telehealth technology and clinical services, providing data-driven recommendations and generating detailed reports for stakeholders.
        Evaluated the efficacy of telehealth platforms and digital interventions to meet the clinical and operational needs of child mental health programs.
        Coordinated cross-functional efforts to develop and enhance applications, programs, and digital tools that support clinical services.
        Fostered partnerships with vendors, healthcare providers, and program partners to optimize service delivery and support statewide telehealth initiatives.
        Interpreted policies, rules, and regulations related to virtual health and telehealth technology.
      
    

    
      
        Project Coordinator I (Technology & Logistics) 
        Sept 2020 – Mar 2022
      
      Texas Child Health Access Through Telemedicine (TCHATT), Texas A&M School of Medicine, College Station, TX
      
        Coordinated program activities under the Telebehavioral Care program with schools, primary care facilities, and other stakeholders through full implementation.
        Facilitated communication and collaboration between the clinic and high-level support.
        Assessed project needs related to communication, referral, management of records, and coordination of services.
        Assisted with forecasting and progress reporting against milestone and budgetary updates.
        Ensured compliance with applicable regulations, including HIPAA and FERPA.
      
    

    
      
        Editorial Coordinator
        Jan 2019 – May 2020
      
      Public Health Post, Boston, MA
      
        Authored article excerpts for the website, newsletter, and social media, engaging over 8 million unique users annually.
        Coordinated with fellows, editors, and marketing and communications coordinators to set production milestones using industry-standard project management tools.
